reason: Applying 'noexec' to /dev/shm prevents binaries from executing in shared memory. While a standard hardening step, many applications (including certain databases, browsers, and container runtimes) rely on executing scripts or binaries from /dev/shm. Applying this blindly can cause application failures.
estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Application failure if software requires execution from /dev/shm; Potential breakage of container runtimes or specialized middleware
